reason_for_decision: The article clearly explains the detector-level difference between cooled and uncooled thermal imaging, turns that difference into practical selection logic, and is strong enough to pass without revision.
strengths: The article explains detector physics in plain language without losing the practical difference between microbolometers and cryocooled detectors.
It makes the trade-off concrete by covering sensitivity, range, startup, power, maintenance, and lifecycle burden together.
The selection section correctly frames the topic as mission fit rather than a simple premium-versus-basic hierarchy.
summary_assessment: This is a strong Foundation article: it is readable, technically grounded, and useful enough to pass without revision.
